description: §65.3.4 — `<program reset="none">` opts out of the built-in reset. The `reset` attribute is a recognized scrml-specific `<program>` directive (attribute-registry.js), so it fires NO W-ATTR-001 unknown-attribute warning and is NOT forwarded to the rendered HTML as a plain attribute; emit-css.ts drops the whole `@layer reset` block (asserted in the source unit test).
rationale: §65.3.4: the reset ships DEFAULT-ON; `reset="none"` is the sole opt-out value. Registering `reset` on `<program>` (closed vocab `["none"]`) suppresses the incidental W-ATTR-001 and marks it a compile-time directive. A clean opt-out compile fires no reset/style diagnostics.
